message:
fix(ft-326s9): populate client_ca_path in bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls (tick 362)

The existing `bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` test constructed a
`client_cfg` with `auth_mode = Mtls` but without `client_ca_path`, then
called `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, ...)`. The bundle builder calls
`build_server_config` internally regardless of which side the bundle is
intended for, and `build_server_config` requires `client_ca_path`
whenever `auth_mode.requires_mtls()` — returning `MissingClientCaPath`
otherwise.

This was a latent contradiction with the test's intent (construct a
working mTLS client bundle + acceptor and exchange data over the wire).
The ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick-337 positive-guard test
(`build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`) explicitly pins
the current production behavior as correct — so the fix is in the test,
not the production code.

Minimal fix (option (a) from ft-326s9 description): populate
`client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path` with the same CA the server uses to
verify client certs. Adds 1 line + a comment explaining why.

Verification:
- `cargo test --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls`: now 1/1 ok (was
  0/1 failing).
- `cargo test --lib distributed::tests::`: 154/154 ok (was 153/154;
  the one failure was this test).

Attribution: ft-326s9 filed tick 361 after discovering the failure in
tick 359. Test was authored by jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit
147b22176) — 8 days before this session. Fix is in the test file
(not production code) and minimal (1 line + 1 comment), so the
"don't disturb other agents' work" AGENTS.md guidance is satisfied:
this is a clear latent-bug fix, not a redirect of a planned solution.

Closes ft-326s9.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
